verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I transmembrane protein with a luminal N-terminal domain. Its primary localization is the early secretory pathway (ER, Golgi, COPII/COPI vesicles). However, documented evidence shows TMED9 reaches the plasma membrane: (1) found in PM baseline surveys, (2) participates in unconventional protein secretion to the PM during ER stress. This constitutes a minority dual-localization pool. The protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM anchor when present at the PM.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Degree to which PM pool is constitutive vs. stress-dependent; quantitative vs. qualitative PM presence under baseline conditions vs. only induced by strong ER stress.
